questions.text: In what year did Nigeria gain independence from Britain?
questions.explanation: Nigeria gained independence from British colonial rule on October 1, 1960.
questions.culturalContext: October 1st is celebrated annually as Nigeria's Independence Day across all 36 states and the FCT.

questions.text: Who became Nigeria's first Prime Minister after independence?
questions.explanation: Sir Abubakar Tafawa Balewa served as Nigeria's first Prime Minister from 1960 to 1966.
questions.culturalContext: Balewa played a key role in negotiating Nigeria's independence and represented the Northern People's Congress.

questions.text: Which Nigerian city served as the capital before Abuja?
questions.explanation: Lagos served as Nigeria's capital until it was officially moved to Abuja in 1991.
questions.culturalContext: Lagos remains Nigeria's economic hub and one of the largest cities in Africa.

questions.text: Nigeria became a republic in which year?
questions.explanation: Nigeria became a republic on October 1, 1963, ending its status as a constitutional monarchy under the British crown.
questions.culturalContext: Nnamdi Azikiwe became Nigeria's first President when the country adopted the republican constitution.

questions.text: What is the name of Nigeria's national anthem adopted in 1978?
questions.explanation: The anthem 'Arise, O Compatriots' replaced the earlier anthem 'Nigeria We Hail Thee' in 1978.
questions.culturalContext: The anthem emphasizes unity, peace, and progress across Nigeria's many ethnic groups.

questions.text: How many states are currently in Nigeria?
questions.explanation: Nigeria currently has 36 states plus the Federal Capital Territory (FCT).
questions.culturalContext: The states were created gradually through several military administrations to improve governance and representation.

questions.text: Which Nigerian leader created the Federal Capital Territory (Abuja)?
questions.explanation: General Murtala Mohammed announced the creation of the Federal Capital Territory in 1976.
questions.culturalContext: Abuja was chosen for its central location and neutrality among Nigeria's ethnic groups.

questions.text: Which war occurred in Nigeria between 1967 and 1970?
questions.explanation: The Nigerian Civil War, also known as the Biafran War, lasted from 1967 to 1970.
questions.culturalContext: The war had lasting social and political impacts on Nigeria's national unity.

questions.explanation: Dr. Nnamdi Azikiwe became Nigeria's first President in 1963 after the country became a republic.
questions.culturalContext: Azikiwe was a key nationalist leader and advocate for African independence.

questions.text: What is the nickname commonly used for Nigeria?
questions.explanation: Nigeria is widely referred to as 'The Giant of Africa' due to its large population and economic influence.
questions.culturalContext: With over 200 million people, Nigeria has the largest population in Africa.
